key_insight: The paper introduces a polyconvex, symmetry-aware framework for anisotropic hyperelasticity that can satisfy standard mechanical constitutive requirements by construction.
engineering_relevance: This enables more reliable constitutive models for complex materials, especially for physics-augmented neural networks used in nonlinear solid mechanics.
method: Builds a new PANN constitutive model using triclinic invariants and group symmetrization.
Derives polyconvex invariants/bases for multiple symmetry classes, including new results for tetragonal and cubic groups.
Uses finite symmetry-group structure so constitutive conditions are enforced a priori.
evidence_signal: The proposed models are benchmarked against highly nonlinear homogenization data from cubic metamaterials.
watch_item: The approach relies on carefully chosen symmetry-group and invariant constructions, so extending it to more complex or imperfect material symmetries may require additional derivations.
